data.title: How to spot a fake crypto investment platform: 10 red flags
data.description:
Key takeawaysFake crypto investment platforms give themselves away in a few obvious ways. Many use fake reviews, incorrect contact information and unrealistic promises to lure you in. The best approach is a healthy dose of skepticism. Watching for these red flags will keep you safe while investing
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/how-to-spot-a-fake-crypto-investment-platform-10-red-flags?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Bitfarms releases Q1 financial report: holds 1,166 Bitcoins
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company Bitfarms announced its Q1 2025 financial report, with revenue reaching $67 million, a year-on-year increase of 33%; Bitcoin mining gross profit margin was 43%, lower than 63% in the same period last year. Net loss for the quarter was $36 million. As of May 13, Bitfarms held approximately $150 million in liquid assets and a total of 1,166 Bitcoins.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin may repeat the 2021 "double top" trend
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Recently, multiple on-chain indicators show that Bitcoin (BTC) may be forming a "double top" pattern similar to that of 2021. The current price is close to the high of about $109,000 set in January, but the weekly RSI has shown a bearish divergence, and trading volume has continued to decline on crypto and institutional platforms. In 2021, Bitcoin quickly fell after reaching a high of about $65,000 in April, and then hit a new high again in November, eventually entering a year-long bear market. Analysts pointed out that although the current market environment is different, the technical indicators are similar to those of that year, and investors should pay attention to potential risks. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Crypto Daybook Americas: Altcoins Rally as ‘Complacent’ Bitcoin Points to Renewed Volatility
data.description:
By Omkar Godbole (All times ET unless indicated otherwise)Investors are still rotating money into altcoins, lifting valuations for ETH, RAY, ENA, MKR and other tokens while leaving bitcoin (BTC) little changed above $100,000.While the biggest cryptocurrency by market value is just 7% short of hitting a new high, its 30-day options-based implied or expected volatility, represented by Deribit's DVOL index, is hovering near the lowest level since June 2024.This type of calm usually precedes big moves. Georgii Verbitskii, a market analyst and founder of TYMIO, a crypto investor app put it best: "Options implied volatility is extremely low, the market doesn't seem to believe in strong moves, and 1- to 3-month option pricing is still cheap
source: https://www.coindesk.com/daybook-us/2025/05/14/crypto-daybook-americas-altcoins-rally-as-complacent-bitcoin-points-to-renewed-volatility

data.title: Bitfarms Reports Increased Revenue Despite Lower Mining Margins in Q1 2025
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Bitfarms has released its financial results for the first quarter of 2025. The company produced 693 BTC during this period, with a direct production cost of $47,800 per BTC and a total cash production cost of $72,300 per BTC. In the first quarter, Bitfarms sold 428 bitcoins.

The company's total revenue for the quarter reached $67 million, marking a 33% increase compared to the same period in 2024. However, the mining gross margin decreased to 43%, down from 63% in the first quarter of 2024.

data.title: MicroStrategy's Bitcoin Holdings Could Propel It to Top Market Cap Position
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Strategy analyst Jeff Walton expressed in a Financial Times documentary titled 'Michael Saylor's $40 Billion Bitcoin Gamble' that MicroStrategy's financial strength, bolstered by its Bitcoin holdings, could eventually lead it to become the top publicly traded company by market capitalization globally. Currently, MicroStrategy possesses approximately 568,840 Bitcoins, valued at $59 billion, which analysts believe will enable it to surpass all other listed companies in the future.

Michael Saylor, featured in the documentary, also stated his belief that Bitcoin's price will exceed one million dollars within the next decade and achieve tenfold growth over the following twenty years.

data.title: Strategy will beat all public equities with Bitcoin, analyst says
data.description:
Michael Saylor’s Strategy, one of the world’s largest corporate holders of Bitcoin, could become the top publicly traded equity one day, according to a Strategy analyst. Strategy will be the “number one publicly traded equity in the entire market” because of its future financial strength enabled with Bitcoin (BTC), Strategy analyst Jeff Walton predicted in the new Financial Times documentary, Michael Saylor’s $40 billion Bitcoin bet. The company currently holds about 568,840 Bitcoin, worth roughly $59 billion, and Walton believes that advantage could push it past all other publicly listed firms in the future. “Strategy holds more of the best assets and the most pristine collateral on the entire planet than any other company, by multiples,” Walton said
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-strategy-number-one-publicly-traded-equity-analyst?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
